SEARCH
Package configs

Class GenericConfig

java.lang.Object
configs.GenericConfig
All Implemented Interfaces:
Config
public class GenericConfig extends Object implements Config
A configuration manager that parses network layout files, validates the resulting logical topology for cyclic paths, and instantiates parallel agents.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

This loader relies on the following framework classes being correctly configured and available in the class path to instantiate and execute the calculation pipeline:

  • Agent - The core interface implemented by loaded agent classes. Target agent classes loaded via reflection must expose a public constructor with the exact signature: public ClassName(String[] subscriptions, String[] publications).
  • ParallelAgent - Thread-isolated concurrent decorator that wraps each loaded agent instance to run callbacks inside a dedicated queue-backed thread.
  • Graph - Graph structure analyzer that inspects topic and agent registrations to verify that no cyclical dependencies exist in the routing topology.
  • TopicManagerSingleton - The singleton state directory resolving the underlying topic references.

Lifecycle Stages:

  1. Parsing and Instantiation: Reads the configuration layout in three-line blocks. It loads implementation classes via reflection, invoking constructors that accept string arrays of subscription and publication topics.
  2. Validation: Constructs a graph representation of the topics and registered components to perform cycle detection. This checks the flow structure to ensure execution safety before processing begins.
  3. Commit and Dispatch: Wraps the validated agents inside concurrent ParallelAgent active objects to handle execution asynchronously on background queues.

File Formatting Specification:

The configuration file expects repeating three-line sets, where empty lines are ignored:

 Line 1: Fully qualified name of the agent class
 Line 2: Comma-separated list of subscription topics (can be empty)
 Line 3: Comma-separated list of publication topics (can be empty)

Example Configuration File (simple.conf):

 graph.PlusAgent
 A,B
 C
 graph.IncAgent
 C
 D

Input Initialization Variations:

Note that different agent classes handle initial cache states differently. For example, in the simple.conf configuration above, PlusAgent initializes its internal values to 0.0 upon construction. Consequently, publishing a message of 1.0 to topic A will immediately trigger an execution output of 1.0 to topic C (calculating 1.0 + 0.0). In contrast, BinOpAgent starts with uninitialized (null) variables and will not output results until both input topics receive their first messages.

Code Integration Example:


 GenericConfig loader = new GenericConfig();
 loader.setConfFile("config_files/simple.conf");
 
 try {
     // Initializes and runs all parallel agents safely after passing cycle validation
     loader.create();
     System.out.println("Processing pipeline successfully deployed.");
 } catch (Exception e) {
     System.err.println("Deployment failed and rolled back: " + e.getMessage());
 }
 
 // Shut down execution pipelines when shutting down the application
 Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook(new Thread(() -> {
     loader.close();
 }));

Rollback Mechanics:

If a cycle is detected, or if reflective instantiation fails, the configuration manager performs an automatic cleanup. Any newly constructed agents are closed, and registered topic states are cleared to maintain consistency and prevent thread leaks.

See Also:
  • Config
  • ParallelAgent
  • TopicManagerSingleton

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Safely terminates all active parallel agents and closes background threads.

    Reads the configuration file, parses the agent specifications, validates the graph for cycles, and dynamically instantiates them.

  • Constructor Details

    • GenericConfig

      public GenericConfig()
      Constructs a GenericConfig instance to handle dynamic agent topology creation.

  • Method Details

    • setConfFile

      public void setConfFile(String confFile)
      Sets the path of the target configuration file.
      Parameters:
      confFile - the system path to the .conf file

    • create

      public void create()
      Reads the configuration file, parses the agent specifications, validates the graph for cycles, and dynamically instantiates them.
      Specified by:
      create in interface Config
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the configuration file path is null or empty, or if the line count is not a multiple of 3
      RuntimeException - if an I/O error occurs, if reflection classes cannot be found, or if a cyclic dependency is detected in the topology

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Retrieves the descriptive, human-readable name of this configuration.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface Config
      Returns:
      the configuration's name string

    • getVersion

      public int getVersion()
      Retrieves the structural release version number of this configuration.
      Specified by:
      getVersion in interface Config
      Returns:
      the configuration's version integer

    • close

      public void close()
      Safely terminates all active parallel agents and closes background threads.
      Specified by:
      close in interface Config
